column1: # DOLCHEO+ encoding TSV — schema per canon/governance/writing-conventions.md
# D, O, L, C rows (6 cols): type
# Tab-separated. Order within file: D/O/L/C/H first, then O-open at end.
# Session: 2026-05-29 design session (one-URL promise, numeric q, transparency echo, parked video model)

column2: Numeric quality override — q is a number, presets are sugar
column3: q accepts an arbitrary integer 0-100 as the source of truth; named presets low/medium/high are sugar pinned to fixed points (images 20/50/80). A caller may pass q=33 to sit between low and medium. Presets remain the documented default; numeric is the power-user/agent override. Extends canon/planning/2026-05-26-url-vocabulary-and-presets.md; does not conflict with it.
column4: Operator Sovee-era practice of a balanced numeric webp q (~33); this-session design discussion
column5: Presets-only (current canon, too coarse for power callers); free-form per-codec quality params (too leaky, breaks the single-knob promise)
column6: working — needs url-vocabulary doc update and worker clamp/validate

column2: Transparency echo — response declares what was resolved and what bound it
column3: Every response carries headers declaring the resolved encode and the binding constraint: X-Encode-Dimension, X-Bound-By (byte-budget | display-target | source-guard), X-Quality (e.g. webp:q=NN). The proxy never blocks on resolution, serves best-effort, discloses what it assumed, and makes the override discoverable from the echo.
column4: Maps to klappy.dev canon principle Partial-Data-With-Transparency-And-Background-Warm; this-session design
column5: Silent resolution (caller cannot tell what bound the output or which param to override)
column6: working — additive, no canon conflict; add to worker response contract

column2: Conversation-only reconstruction diverges from canon until the repo is read
column3: An assistant designing from chat alone reproduced the exact source-as-ceiling error canon already warns about, used a discrete resolution ladder instead of the documented target*1.5 mod-16 overshoot, and invented device/bandwidth/stutter triggers absent from v1 scope. All three were corrected only after pointing oddkit at the repo. Mechanism: pattern-matching backfills gaps with plausible-but-wrong priors; canon is the ground truth, not the reconstruction.
column4: This session; matches the bugbot meta-lesson in canon/handoffs/2026-05-27-encode-arithmetic-wrong-turn.md

column2: Land numeric-q + echo into URL vocab; keep video model parked
column3: Fold the numeric-q override and the transparency-echo headers into canon/planning/2026-05-26-url-vocabulary-and-presets.md and the worker response contract. Leave the video three-trigger model as the parked O-open below until video scope opens. Commit this encodings file into canon/encodings/.

column4: Video adaptive model (3 triggers + agent courier) — revisit when video un-defers
column5: Forward model borrowed from Sovee adaptive-streaming days: resolution as a single output lever driven by three triggers — screen (Client Hints), bandwidth, and compute/stutter as runtime insurance — with agents acting as thin couriers that forward the END USER's request signals, never the agent runtime's own User-Agent. CONFLICTS with v1 canon, where the caller specifies width/q explicitly and the proxy does not sniff the device. Parked; closes via a planning doc + gate when video scope opens.
